expectations.evidence: Document opens with '## Agent Design Reading' section (line 3) containing all standard fields: user signal, target agent behavior, evidence available, context needed, model decision, tools/actions, feedback loop, verification gate, and weakest layer (lines 1-54).

expectations.expectation: Identifies weakest layer (context assembly or timing judgment)
expectations.evidence: Lines 49-54 explicitly state 'Current weakest layer: LAYER 2 — Context Assembly' with rationale: no mechanism to detect good vs. bad intervention moments, reacts to window title changes after-the-fact rather than reasoning about user flow state. Root cause analysis (Problem A/B/C, lines 58-103) and Architecture Correction section (lines 255-286) further elaborate the timing judgment deficiency.

expectations.expectation: Produces a Claude Code work order, not redesign memory system
expectations.evidence: Lines 289-306 contain 'Summary of Changes Needed' table with 7 prioritized file-level changes (window_monitor.rs, main_loop.rs, prompt_context.rs, model_client.rs, commands.rs, runtime_state.rs, notification_manager.rs), each with specific concrete changes. Ends with verification command 'cd cozmio && cargo build'. No memory system redesign proposed — changes are targeted at the intervention timing/notification flow.

notes: All three expectations met. The output correctly uses the Agent Design Reading format, identifies LAYER 2 (Context Assembly) as the weakest layer with thorough root cause analysis, and concludes with a concrete, file-specific work order targeting the intervention timing problem rather than proposing a memory system overhaul.
